steps.title: Target the false claim
steps.content: The claim to refute says malapportionment favors small urban districts. The notes say the opposite: the formula gives land extra weight, so less populated rural districts with large land areas receive disproportionate representation.

steps.title: Reject unsupported answers
steps.content: B repeats the false idea that rural districts are underrepresented. C says more populated districts are overrepresented, contradicting the notes. D only defines malapportionment and does not identify which districts benefit. The answer is A.
